source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Fall from height through open hole
actions_taken: IP evacuated via helicopter to onshore medical facility
Area secured to prevent further access
Permanent grating installed over open hole
Access ladder removed to prevent reoccurrence
BSEE Houma District notified and onsite investigation conducted
event.summary: On March 28, 2024, a Transocean Subsea Superintendent fell approximately nine feet through an open hole in a BOP work platform while performing maintenance on the Deepwater Atlas drillship. The hatch cover securing the hole had been damaged and removed during a pre-lift check without proper communication or barriers being installed. The IP was wearing a safety harness but was not tied off. He sustained a knee injury and abrasions and was evacuated for medical treatment. The incident was caused by failure to communicate the open hole, flawed hatch cover design, and lack of proper hazard analysis and barriers.
recommendations: Implement proper job task hazard analysis for all subsea BOP maintenance activities
Ensure all personnel are informed of open holes and hazards before work begins
Redesign hatch cover hinge to prevent separation and dropped object hazard
Enforce fall protection tie-off requirements for all work at heights
Improve communication protocols between lifting teams and work crews
Ensure procedures include hazard identification, mitigations, and personnel sign-offs

hazards.name: Damaged hatch cover with separated hinge
Work at height on BOP platform

threats.name: Failure to communicate open hole to all personnel
Hatch cover hinge design failure causing separation
Removal of hatch cover without barrier installation
Failure to tie off fall protection harness

consequences.name: Injury to personnel (knee injury and abrasions)
Dropped object hazard (remote control box, hatch cover)
Evacuation and loss of operational time

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for subsea BOP maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and required mitigations before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent hazards from being overlooked

supporting_text: The inspectors requested the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the subsea BOP maintenance job task, but it could not be provided.
If a job task hazard analysis was completed the open hole would have been recognized as a hazard and mitigations would have been put in place.

controls.name: Hazard Identification Prompt Card
controls.barrier_role: Identify specific hazards for the work task
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prompt hazard recognition

supporting_text: Although a Hazard Identification Prompt Card was documented, it did not identify the open hole.

controls.name: Communication of hazards to all personnel
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all team members are aware of open hole and required precautions
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent personnel from entering hazardous areas unaware

supporting_text: The roustabout on the lifting team did not communicate the open hole to all members of the Subsea BOP team after removing the damaged hatch cover.
The open hole was not communicated to all personnel involved in the job.
Failure to communicate the open hole led the IP to believe he could enter the work platform without tying off his harness.

controls.name: Hatch cover design and hinge security
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hatch cover separation and dropped object hazard
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to prevent equipment failure

supporting_text: The hinge securing the hatch cover to the work platform separated.
Poor design of the hinge connecting the hatch cover to the work platform. The hinge separated from the hatch cover causing the hatch cover to become a possible dropped object.
The design of the work platform hatch cover led to a possible dropped object anytime it was lifted because it was not secured.

controls.name: Barrier installation before hatch cover removal
controls.barrier_role: Prevent access to open hole during maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent fall hazard

supporting_text: Access to the open hole was not prevented.
Failure to put barriers in place prior to removal of hatch cover.
Removing damaged hatch cover without a barricade in place

controls.name: Fall protection harness tie-off requirement
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from falling or limit fall distance
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment to mitigate fall consequences

supporting_text: Although the IP was wearing a safety harness, he was not tied off.
Failure to tie off fall protection.
Work Environment – PPE - Failure to tie off fall protection.

controls.name: Procedures for working at heights on BOP equipment
controls.barrier_role: Provide guidance for safe work practices at height
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to establish safe work practices

supporting_text: Procedures were available but did not have hazard identification, hazard mitigations or a place for signatures for personnel performing the work.
The IP failed to address the no drop tool policy addressed in the procedures for 'Working at Heights on BOP / Related Equipment or in Subsea areas'.
Not following company procedures

controls.name: Permanent grating installation over open hole
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fall through open hole after incident
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier installed post-incident to prevent recurrence

supporting_text: Permanent grating was installed over the open hole and the access ladder was removed to prevent reoccurrence of this incident.
New grating was installed over the hole where the IP fell to ensure the safety of the work platform and prevent further incidents.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
